title: Letters to the Editor
summary: Conoco Phillips Alaska Inc. has permitted a new well in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska, the Carbon No. 1, according to Petroleum News. The well is five miles north-northwest of Conoco's Spark discovery well, one of five wells and one sidetrack that Conoco and partner Anadarko Petroleum announced as discoveries in May 2001. The companies said they did extensive testing at the Spark drilling location, and the Spark No. 1A well tested 1,550 barrels per day of liquid hydrocarbons and 26.5 million cubic feet per day of natural gas. Alaska bankruptcies set record The U.S. Bankruptcy Court based in Alaska opened a record number of cases in 2003. The 1,472 new cases topped the record 1,463 set in 1998. title: Letters to the Editor
summary: Operators of Greens Creek, an underground mine on Admiralty Island that employs about 260 people, proposed to expand the tailings disposal area to accommodate more ore reserves. Tailings are what's left of the material removed from the mine after the metal has been extracted. The tailings site now covers 23.2 acres and is permitted to expand to 29 acres. Operators say that space will last two more years. The mine asked that it be allowed to expand the site to 61.3 acres, allowing enough room for 22 years' worth of tailings if the mine continues at its current pace. The alternative approved by the Forest Service allows the 61.3-acre expansion but shifts it to the east in order to minimize the impact on monument land. summary: Help is available Thursday at these times and locations in Anchorage: 9 a.m.-3:30 p.m., Anchorage Senior Center, 1300 E. 19th Ave.; 6-9 p.m., St. Anthony's Catholic Church, 825 S.
